# Domain Glossary

Maps business terms to Rust types. This is the ubiquitous language of our todo list
application. Code should read like these definitions -- if the business says "complete
a todo," the code says `complete_todo()`.

Maintained by the mob. Changes reviewed by the domain architect (Scott Wlaschin).

---

## Core Domain Types

| Domain Term | Rust Type | Module | Notes |
|-------------|-----------|--------|-------|
| User | `User` | `domain` | An authenticated user with valid credentials |
| User ID | `UserId(Uuid)` | `domain` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a user |
| Email address | `ValidatedEmail` | `domain` | Constructed via `ValidatedEmail::parse()`; validated format, normalized to lowercase (case-insensitive per US-2) |
| Password | (not stored as domain type) | -- | Hashed with Argon2 at the boundary; the domain never sees raw passwords |
| Todo item | `TodoItem` (enum) | `domain` | Sum type: `Pending` or `Completed` variant. No boolean flags. |
| Todo item ID | `TodoItemId(Uuid)` | `domain` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a todo item |
| Todo title | `TodoTitle` | `domain` | Non-empty, max 300 chars (per US-5), trimmed; constructed via `TodoTitle::parse()` |
| Pending todo | `TodoItem::Pending { ... }` | `domain` | A todo that has not been completed; has title and created_at |
| Completed todo | `TodoItem::Completed { ... }` | `domain` | A todo that has been completed; has title, created_at, and completed_at |

## Domain Actions (Ubiquitous Language)

| Business Action | Rust Function / Method | Notes |
|-----------------|----------------------|-------|
| Register a user | `register_user()` | Service: creates user with validated email and hashed password |
| Log in | `authenticate_user()` | Service: verifies credentials; route stores user_id in session |
| Log out | `post_logout()` | Route: flushes session, redirects to login |
| Add a todo | `add_todo()` | Service: parses title, creates `TodoItem::Pending`, persists |
| View todos | `get_todos()` | Service: fetches all todos for authenticated user |
| Toggle completion | `toggle_todo_completion()` | Service: looks up todo, verifies ownership, toggles state |
| Complete a todo | `TodoItem::complete()` | Domain method: Pending -> Completed; records completion time |
| Uncomplete a todo | `TodoItem::uncomplete()` | Domain method: Completed -> Pending; drops completion time |
| Delete a todo | `delete_todo()` | Removes a todo item from the user's list |
| Edit a todo title | `edit_todo_title()` | Updates the title on an existing todo item |

## Domain Errors

input.old_string: | Delete a todo | `delete_todo()` | Removes a todo item from the user's list |
input.new_string: | Delete a todo | `delete_todo()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes from DB |

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Domain Glossary

Maps business terms to Rust types. This is the ubiquitous language of our todo list
application. Code should read like these definitions -- if the business says "complete
a todo," the code says `complete_todo()`.

Maintained by the mob. Changes reviewed by the domain architect (Scott Wlaschin).

---

## Core Domain Types

| Domain Term | Rust Type | Module | Notes |
|-------------|-----------|--------|-------|
| User | `User` | `domain` | An authenticated user with valid credentials |
| User ID | `UserId(Uuid)` | `domain` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a user |
| Email address | `ValidatedEmail` | `domain` | Constructed via `ValidatedEmail::parse()`; validated format, normalized to lowercase (case-insensitive per US-2) |
| Password | (not stored as domain type) | -- | Hashed with Argon2 at the boundary; the domain never sees raw passwords |
| Todo item | `TodoItem` (enum) | `domain` | Sum type: `Pending` or `Completed` variant. No boolean flags. |
| Todo item ID | `TodoItemId(Uuid)` | `domain` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a todo item |
| Todo title | `TodoTitle` | `domain` | Non-empty, max 300 chars (per US-5), trimmed; constructed via `TodoTitle::parse()` |
| Pending todo | `TodoItem::Pending { ... }` | `domain` | A todo that has not been completed; has title and created_at |
| Completed todo | `TodoItem::Completed { ... }` | `domain` | A todo that has been completed; has title, created_at, and completed_at |

## Domain Actions (Ubiquitous Language)

| Business Action | Rust Function / Method | Notes |
|-----------------|----------------------|-------|
| Register a user | `register_user()` | Service: creates user with validated email and hashed password |
| Log in | `authenticate_user()` | Service: verifies credentials; route stores user_id in session |
| Log out | `post_logout()` | Route: flushes session, redirects to login |
| Add a todo | `add_todo()` | Service: parses title, creates `TodoItem::Pending`, persists |
| View todos | `get_todos()` | Service: fetches all todos for authenticated user |
| Toggle completion | `toggle_todo_completion()` | Service: looks up todo, verifies ownership, toggles state |
| Complete a todo | `TodoItem::complete()` | Domain method: Pending -> Completed; records completion time |
| Uncomplete a todo | `TodoItem::uncomplete()` | Domain method: Completed -> Pending; drops completion time |
| Delete a todo | `delete_todo()` | Removes a todo item from the user's list |
| Edit a todo title | `edit_todo_title()` | Updates the title on an existing todo item |

## Domain Errors

| Error Condition | Rust Type | Notes |
|-----------------|-----------|-------|
| Title is empty | `TodoTitleError::Empty` | Todo title cannot be blank |
| Title too long | `TodoTitleError::TooLong { max, actual }` | Exceeds maximum allowed length |
| Todo not found | `ToggleTodoError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
| Not authorized | `ToggleTodoError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
| Email invalid | `RegistrationError::InvalidEmail(EmailValidationError)` | Email fails format validation |
| Duplicate email | `RegistrationError::DuplicateEmail` | Registration attempted with existing email |
| Invalid credentials | `AuthenticationError::InvalidCredentials` | Login failed (generic -- no info leak) |
| Invalid title | `AddTodoError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in add_todo service |

## Error Copy Convention

Domain error messages (`#[error("...")]`) describe **what invariant was violated** -- they
are written for developers and logs. User-facing error messages are **instructions** that
tell the user what to fix -- they are written for humans scanning a form.

The route/template layer translates domain errors into user-facing copy. Templates never
call `error.to_string()` on a domain error.

| Domain Error | User-Facing Copy |
|-------------|-----------------|
| `EmailValidationError::Empty` | "Enter your email address" |
| `EmailValidationError::MissingAtSymbol` | "Enter a valid email address, like name@example.com" |
| `EmailValidationError::TooLong` | "That email address is too long" |
| `PasswordError::Empty` | "Enter a password" |
| `PasswordError::TooShort` | "Your password needs at least 8 characters" |
| `PasswordError::TooLong` | "That password is too long" |
| `RegistrationError::DuplicateEmail` | "Unable to create account. If you already have an account, try signing in." |
| `AuthenticationError::InvalidCredentials` | "That email or password didn't work. Try again." |
| `TodoTitleError::Empty` | (silently ignored per US-5 -- empty submissions are not errors) |
| `TodoTitleError::TooLong` | "That title is too long (max 300 characters)" |

*Copy reviewed by Steve Krug. Update this table when adding new error types.*

---

## Type Design Principles

These principles govern how we model the domain:

1. **Parse, don't validate**: `TodoTitle::parse(input) -> Result<TodoTitle, TodoError>`
   not `fn is_valid_title(input: &str) -> bool`. Parsing produces a typed value that
   carries proof of validity. Validate once at the boundary; never re-validate inside.

2. **Make illegal states unrepresentable**: A `TodoItem` is either `Pending` or
   `Completed` -- never both, never neither. The enum enforces this at compile time.
   No boolean `is_completed` flag with an `Option<completed_at>` that could be
   inconsistent.

3. **Newtypes for everything**: `UserId` is not a bare `Uuid`. `ValidatedEmail` is
   not a bare `String`. The type system prevents mixing them up. These are zero-cost
   abstractions in Rust.

4. **Pure domain core**: Everything in `src/domain/` is pure functions and types. No
   database imports, no HTTP types, no I/O. If you need a mock to test domain logic,
   I/O has leaked into the domain.

5. **Workflows as type-safe pipelines**:
   ```
   UnvalidatedInput -> ValidatedInput -> DomainResult
   ```
   Each step's output type is the next step's input. You cannot skip validation.

## State Machine: TodoItem

```
                 add_todo()
  [nothing] ────────────────> Pending
                                 │  ^
               item.complete()   │  │  item.uncomplete()
                                 │  │
                                 v  │
                             Completed
```

Both `Pending` and `Completed` variants can be deleted (`delete_todo()`).
The `edit_todo_title()` action applies to both states.
Completion is **reversible** -- users can toggle between Pending and Completed (US-6).

lines: | Toggle completion | `toggle_todo_completion()` | Service: looks up todo, verifies ownership, toggles state |
| Complete a todo | `TodoItem::complete()` | Domain method: Pending -> Completed; records completion time |
| Uncomplete a todo | `TodoItem::uncomplete()` | Domain method: Completed -> Pending; drops completion time |
-| Delete a todo | `delete_todo()` | Removes a todo item from the user's list |
+| Delete a todo | `delete_todo()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes from DB |
| Edit a todo title | `edit_todo_title()` | Updates the title on an existing todo item |

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Domain Glossary

Maps business terms to Rust types. This is the ubiquitous language of our todo list
application. Code should read like these definitions -- if the business says "complete
a todo," the code says `complete_todo()`.

Maintained by the mob. Changes reviewed by the domain architect (Scott Wlaschin).

---

## Core Domain Types

| Domain Term | Rust Type | Module | Notes |
|-------------|-----------|--------|-------|
| User | `User` | `domain` | An authenticated user with valid credentials |
| User ID | `UserId(Uuid)` | `domain` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a user |
| Email address | `ValidatedEmail` | `domain` | Constructed via `ValidatedEmail::parse()`; validated format, normalized to lowercase (case-insensitive per US-2) |
| Password | (not stored as domain type) | -- | Hashed with Argon2 at the boundary; the domain never sees raw passwords |
| Todo item | `TodoItem` (enum) | `domain` | Sum type: `Pending` or `Completed` variant. No boolean flags. |
| Todo item ID | `TodoItemId(Uuid)` | `domain` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a todo item |
| Todo title | `TodoTitle` | `domain` | Non-empty, max 300 chars (per US-5), trimmed; constructed via `TodoTitle::parse()` |
| Pending todo | `TodoItem::Pending { ... }` | `domain` | A todo that has not been completed; has title and created_at |
| Completed todo | `TodoItem::Completed { ... }` | `domain` | A todo that has been completed; has title, created_at, and completed_at |

## Domain Actions (Ubiquitous Language)

| Business Action | Rust Function / Method | Notes |
|-----------------|----------------------|-------|
| Register a user | `register_user()` | Service: creates user with validated email and hashed password |
| Log in | `authenticate_user()` | Service: verifies credentials; route stores user_id in session |
| Log out | `post_logout()` | Route: flushes session, redirects to login |
| Add a todo | `add_todo()` | Service: parses title, creates `TodoItem::Pending`, persists |
| View todos | `get_todos()` | Service: fetches all todos for authenticated user |
| Toggle completion | `toggle_todo_completion()` | Service: looks up todo, verifies ownership, toggles state |
| Complete a todo | `TodoItem::complete()` | Domain method: Pending -> Completed; records completion time |
| Uncomplete a todo | `TodoItem::uncomplete()` | Domain method: Completed -> Pending; drops completion time |
| Delete a todo | `delete_todo()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes from DB |
| Edit a todo title | `edit_todo_title()` | Updates the title on an existing todo item |

## Domain Errors

| Error Condition | Rust Type | Notes |
|-----------------|-----------|-------|
| Title is empty | `TodoTitleError::Empty` | Todo title cannot be blank |
| Title too long | `TodoTitleError::TooLong { max, actual }` | Exceeds maximum allowed length |
| Todo not found | `ToggleTodoError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
| Not authorized | `ToggleTodoError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
| Email invalid | `RegistrationError::InvalidEmail(EmailValidationError)` | Email fails format validation |
| Duplicate email | `RegistrationError::DuplicateEmail` | Registration attempted with existing email |
| Invalid credentials | `AuthenticationError::InvalidCredentials` | Login failed (generic -- no info leak) |
| Invalid title | `AddTodoError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in add_todo service |

## Error Copy Convention

Domain error messages (`#[error("...")]`) describe **what invariant was violated** -- they
are written for developers and logs. User-facing error messages are **instructions** that
tell the user what to fix -- they are written for humans scanning a form.

The route/template layer translates domain errors into user-facing copy. Templates never
call `error.to_string()` on a domain error.

| Domain Error | User-Facing Copy |
|-------------|-----------------|
| `EmailValidationError::Empty` | "Enter your email address" |
| `EmailValidationError::MissingAtSymbol` | "Enter a valid email address, like name@example.com" |
| `EmailValidationError::TooLong` | "That email address is too long" |
| `PasswordError::Empty` | "Enter a password" |
| `PasswordError::TooShort` | "Your password needs at least 8 characters" |
| `PasswordError::TooLong` | "That password is too long" |
| `RegistrationError::DuplicateEmail` | "Unable to create account. If you already have an account, try signing in." |
| `AuthenticationError::InvalidCredentials` | "That email or password didn't work. Try again." |
| `TodoTitleError::Empty` | (silently ignored per US-5 -- empty submissions are not errors) |
| `TodoTitleError::TooLong` | "That title is too long (max 300 characters)" |

*Copy reviewed by Steve Krug. Update this table when adding new error types.*

---

## Type Design Principles

These principles govern how we model the domain:

1. **Parse, don't validate**: `TodoTitle::parse(input) -> Result<TodoTitle, TodoError>`
   not `fn is_valid_title(input: &str) -> bool`. Parsing produces a typed value that
   carries proof of validity. Validate once at the boundary; never re-validate inside.

2. **Make illegal states unrepresentable**: A `TodoItem` is either `Pending` or
   `Completed` -- never both, never neither. The enum enforces this at compile time.
   No boolean `is_completed` flag with an `Option<completed_at>` that could be
   inconsistent.

3. **Newtypes for everything**: `UserId` is not a bare `Uuid`. `ValidatedEmail` is
   not a bare `String`. The type system prevents mixing them up. These are zero-cost
   abstractions in Rust.

4. **Pure domain core**: Everything in `src/domain/` is pure functions and types. No
   database imports, no HTTP types, no I/O. If you need a mock to test domain logic,
   I/O has leaked into the domain.

5. **Workflows as type-safe pipelines**:
   ```
   UnvalidatedInput -> ValidatedInput -> DomainResult
   ```
   Each step's output type is the next step's input. You cannot skip validation.

## State Machine: TodoItem

```
                 add_todo()
  [nothing] ────────────────> Pending
                                 │  ^
               item.complete()   │  │  item.uncomplete()
                                 │  │
                                 v  │
                             Completed
```

Both `Pending` and `Completed` variants can be deleted (`delete_todo()`).
The `edit_todo_title()` action applies to both states.
Completion is **reversible** -- users can toggle between Pending and Completed (US-6).

| Title too long | `TodoTitleError::TooLong { max, actual }` | Exceeds maximum allowed length |

-| Not authorized | `ToggleTodoError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
+| Todo not found (toggle) | `ToggleTodoError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
+| Not authorized (toggle) | `ToggleTodoError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
+| Todo not found (delete) | `DeleteTodoError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
+| Not authorized (delete) | `DeleteTodoError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |

| Duplicate email | `RegistrationError::DuplicateEmail` | Registration attempted with existing email |
| Invalid credentials | `AuthenticationError::InvalidCredentials` | Login failed (generic -- no info leak) |

Task #9 (Delete a todo item) status update. All quality gates GREEN (fmt, clippy, 64 tests).

**Changes I made for Task #9:**
1. Wired up delete route: `.route("/todos/{id}/delete", axum::routing::post(post_delete_todo))` in startup.rs (using Axum 0.8 `{id}` syntax)
2. Added delete button to template: `<form method="post" action="/todos/{{ todo.id }}/delete">` with `<button aria-label="Delete "{{ todo.title }}"">` -- per US-7 (button not link for destructive action, accessible label)
3. Fixed `cargo fmt` issue in `todo_service.rs`
4. Updated glossary with `DeleteTodoError` variants and `delete_todo()` service description

**Domain review of Task #9 code -- CONSENT:**

1. **`delete_todo()` service** -- Correct pattern: find by ID, verify ownership (user_id match), then delete. Same error pattern as toggle: `NotFound`, `Unauthorized`, `Unexpected`.

2. **`delete_todo()` repository** -- Simple `DELETE FROM todos WHERE id = $1`. Clean.

3. **`post_delete_todo` route** -- Same structure as toggle route: Path<Uuid> extractor, ownership check via service, 404/403 error responses, redirect to /todos on success.

4. **Template delete button** -- Uses multiplication sign character (U+2715) for the X icon. Has `aria-label` with todo title. Works without JS (form POST).

**What still needs doing for Task #9:**
- Integration tests for delete (POST /todos/{id}/delete)
- Per US-7: confirmation mechanism before deletion ("keep it lightweight, not a modal dialog") -- this could be a JS enhancement or a simple confirm/cancel toggle
- After deletion, focus management (focus to next item or to add input)

The integration test suite for delete should mirror the toggle tests: happy path delete, delete nonexistent (404), delete another user's todo (403), unauthenticated delete (redirect), delete last item transitions to empty state, and verify the delete button has an accessible label.

Analysis: Let me chronologically analyze the conversation to build a comprehensive summary. 1. **Context Reset**: This session was continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. I was spawned as Scott Wlaschin (Domain Architect) on a 9-person ensemble team building a Rust todo list app. The summary from the previous session was provided. 2. **Initial State Check**: I checked git status (clean, up to date), read the current state of files (register.rs, register.html, ci.yml, user-stories.md, flake.nix), and verified the a11y fixes from the previous session were already committed (commit 6c757bc). 3. **Task 7 Work (View todo list + Add todo item)**: - Found uncommitted Task #7 files created by teammates (todo_title.rs, todo_item.rs, todo_repository.rs, auth.rs, todos.rs, todo_service.rs, todos.html, migration) - Ran migrations via nix develop - Fixed clippy issues: added `Default` impl for `TodoItemId`, added `#[allow(dead_code)]` on `TodoItemView::id` - Reviewed domain types (TodoTitle, TodoItem, TodoItemId, TodoRecord) - Updated glossary with correct error type names - Sent domain review to team-lead - All 53 tests passed (25 unit + 28 integration) - Then working tree went clean - teammates committed Task #7 4. **Task 8 Work (Complete a todo item)**: - Teammates added `complete()` and `uncomplete()` methods to TodoItem - Fixed critical Axum 0.8 path syntax: `/todos/:id/toggle` -> `/todos/{id}/toggle` - Removed unnecessary `#[allow(dead_code)]` on TodoItemView::id (template now uses it) - Teammates added toggle service, repository, route handler, and template - All 64 tests passed (30 unit + 34 integration) - Updated glossary with toggle-related entries 5. **Task 9 Work (Delete a todo item)**: - Teammates created delete_todo service, repository, and route handler - I wired up the delete route in startup.rs: `/todos/{id}/delete` - Added delete button to template with accessible aria-label - Fixed cargo fmt issue in todo_service.rs - Updated glossary with DeleteTodoError variants - All 64 tests pass - Task committed by teammates (commit 9612b63) Key errors fixed: - Axum 0.8 path parameter syntax (`:id` -> `{id}`) - SQLx chrono/time type mismatch (teammates used type overrides) - Missing `Default` impl for `TodoItemId` (clippy) - Various `cargo fmt` issues - Unused import warnings during incremental development The user messages in this session are system-generated context from the previous conversation's summary and tool results. The actual "user" in this context is the team-lead agent coordinating work. Current state: Working tree is clean, branch up to date. Task #9 (Delete) is in_progress (commit 9612b63 "Add delete todo route, service, and repository (Task 9 WIP)"), Task #20 (Edit) is also in_progress. Integration tests for delete still needed. Summary: 1. Primary Request and Intent: I was spawned as **Scott Wlaschin (Domain Architect)** on a 9-person ensemble team building a multi-user todo list app in Rust. This session continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. My responsibilities are: - Guide domain modeling with type-driven design (parse-don't-validate, make illegal states unrepresentable) - Maintain the domain glossary (`docs/glossary.md`) - Review code for domain purity violations - Fix compilation errors, clippy warnings, and Axum 0.8 compatibility issues - Participate in mob consensus decisions and report to team-lead The session covered Tasks #7 (View/Add todos), #8 (Complete/Uncomplete todos), and #9 (Delete todos). I reviewed domain code, fixed critical bugs, wired up routes, added template elements, and kept the glossary synchronized. 2. Key Technical Concepts: - **Type-driven domain modeling**: Parse-don't-validate, newtypes (UserId, TodoItemId, TodoTitle, ValidatedEmail), enum state machines (TodoItem::Pending/Completed) - **Axum 0.8 compatibility**: Path parameters use `{id}` syntax NOT `:id` (breaking change from 0.7). Native async traits (no `#[axum::async_trait]` needed for `FromRequestParts`). - **SQLx chrono/time type mismatch**: When both `time` and `chrono` crates are present, SQLx defaults to `time::OffsetDateTime`. Fix: use type overrides in queries like `completed_at as "completed_at: DateTime"` - **Three-layer architecture**: Domain (pure types) -> Service (orchestration, tracing) -> Route (HTTP, user-facing error copy) - **Error copy convention**: Domain `#[error("...")]` for devs/logs; route layer translates to user-facing instructions - **Ensemble/mob programming**: Multiple agent teammates writing code concurrently, requiring frequent re-reads of files before editing - **Commit discipline**: Commit on every green step, CI gate before new work, clean working tree at task boundaries - **Progressive enhancement**: HTML forms work without JS, HTMX enhances later - **Accessibility patterns**: `aria-label` on action buttons with todo title context, `role="alert"` for errors, `role="list"` on `
    ` 3. Files and Code Sections: - **`/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/src/domain/todo_item.rs`** (created by teammates, modified by me) - Core domain type implementing TodoItem enum state machine - I added `Default` impl for `TodoItemId` to fix clippy `new_without_default` - Teammates added `complete()` and `uncomplete()` methods for Task #8 - Contains 7 unit tests (2 original + 5 for complete/uncomplete) ```rust impl Default for TodoItemId { fn default() -> Self { Self::new() } } pub fn complete(self) -> Self { match self { Self::Pending { id, user_id, title, created_at } => Self::Completed { id, user_id, title, created_at, completed_at: Utc::now(), }, completed @ Self::Completed { .. } => completed, } } pub fn uncomplete(self) -> Self { match self { Self::Completed { id, user_id, title, created_at, .. } => Self::Pending { id, user_id, title, created_at, }, pending @ Self::Pending { .. } => pending, } } ``` - **`/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/src/domain/todo_title.rs`** (created by teammates) - Parse-don't-validate newtype, max 300 chars, trims whitespace, 7 unit tests - Clean domain type, no modifications needed from me - **`/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/src/startup.rs`** (modified by me multiple times) - Fixed Axum 0.8 path syntax: `:id` -> `{id}` - Wired up toggle route: `/todos/{id}/toggle` - Wired up delete route: `/todos/{id}/delete` ```rust .route("/todos", axum::routing::get(get_todos_page).post(post_todo)) .route("/todos/{id}/toggle", axum::routing::post(post_toggle_todo)) .route("/todos/{id}/delete", axum::routing::post(post_delete_todo)) ``` - **`/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/src/routes/todos.rs`** (modified by me and teammates) - Removed `#[allow(dead_code)]` from `TodoItemView::id` after template started using it - Contains `get_todos_page`, `post_todo`, `post_toggle_todo`, `post_delete_todo` handlers - Error handling follows the three-layer pattern with `TodosError` ```rust pub async fn post_delete_todo( AuthenticatedUser(user_id): AuthenticatedUser, State(pool): State, Path(todo_id): Path, ) -> Result { let todo_item_id = TodoItemId::from_uuid(todo_id); match delete_todo(&pool, &todo_item_id, &user_id).await { Ok(()) => Ok(Redirect::to("/todos").into_response()), Err(DeleteTodoError::NotFound) => Ok((StatusCode::NOT_FOUND, Html("

    Todo not found

    ".to_string())).into_response()), Err(DeleteTodoError::Unauthorized) => Ok((StatusCode::FORBIDDEN, Html("

    Not authorized

    ".to_string())).into_response()), Err(DeleteTodoError::Unexpected(err)) => Err(TodosError::Unexpected(err)), } } ``` - **`/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/src/routes/auth.rs`** (created by teammates) - `AuthenticatedUser` extractor using `FromRequestParts` (native async trait, no `#[axum::async_trait]`) - Redirects to `/login` if unauthenticated - **`/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/src/services/todo_service.rs`** (created by teammates, formatted by me) - Contains `add_todo`, `get_todos`, `toggle_todo_completion`, `delete_todo` services - Each service verifies ownership (user_id match) before mutations - Fixed `cargo fmt` issue in `delete_todo` function - **`/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/src/infrastructure/todo_repository.rs`** (created by teammates) - Uses SQLx type overrides for chrono compatibility: `completed_at as "completed_at: DateTime"` - Functions: `insert_todo`, `find_todos_by_user`, `find_todo_by_id`, `update_todo_completion`, `delete_todo` - **`/home/jwilger/projects/todo_list/templates/todos.html`** (created by teammates, modified by me) - I added the delete button with accessible label ```html {{ todo.title }}

# Domain Glossary

Maps business terms to Rust types. This is the ubiquitous language of our todo list
application. Code should read like these definitions -- if the business says "complete
a todo," the code says `complete_todo()`.

Maintained by the mob. Changes reviewed by the domain architect (Scott Wlaschin).

---

## Core Domain Types

| Domain Term | Rust Type | Module | Notes |
|-------------|-----------|--------|-------|
| User | `User` | `domain` | An authenticated user with valid credentials |
| User ID | `UserId(Uuid)` | `domain` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a user |
| Email address | `ValidatedEmail` | `domain` | Constructed via `ValidatedEmail::parse()`; validated format, normalized to lowercase (case-insensitive per US-2) |
| Password | (not stored as domain type) | -- | Hashed with Argon2 at the boundary; the domain never sees raw passwords |
| Todo item | `TodoItem` (enum) | `domain` | Sum type: `Pending` or `Completed` variant. No boolean flags. |
| Todo item ID | `TodoItemId(Uuid)` | `domain` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a todo item |
| Todo title | `TodoTitle` | `domain` | Non-empty, max 300 chars (per US-5), trimmed; constructed via `TodoTitle::parse()` |
| Pending todo | `TodoItem::Pending { ... }` | `domain` | A todo that has not been completed; has title and created_at |
| Completed todo | `TodoItem::Completed { ... }` | `domain` | A todo that has been completed; has title, created_at, and completed_at |

## Domain Actions (Ubiquitous Language)

| Business Action | Rust Function / Method | Notes |
|-----------------|----------------------|-------|
| Register a user | `register_user()` | Service: creates user with validated email and hashed password |
| Log in | `authenticate_user()` | Service: verifies credentials; route stores user_id in session |
| Log out | `post_logout()` | Route: flushes session, redirects to login |
| Add a todo | `add_todo()` | Service: parses title, creates `TodoItem::Pending`, persists |
| View todos | `get_todos()` | Service: fetches all todos for authenticated user |
| Toggle completion | `toggle_todo_completion()` | Service: looks up todo, verifies ownership, toggles state |
| Complete a todo | `TodoItem::complete()` | Domain method: Pending -> Completed; records completion time |
| Uncomplete a todo | `TodoItem::uncomplete()` | Domain method: Completed -> Pending; drops completion time |
| Delete a todo | `delete_todo()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes from DB |
| Edit a todo title | `edit_todo_title()` | Updates the title on an existing todo item |

## Domain Errors

| Error Condition | Rust Type | Notes |
|-----------------|-----------|-------|
| Title is empty | `TodoTitleError::Empty` | Todo title cannot be blank |
| Title too long | `TodoTitleError::TooLong { max, actual }` | Exceeds maximum allowed length |
| Todo not found (toggle) | `ToggleTodoError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
| Not authorized (toggle) | `ToggleTodoError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
| Todo not found (delete) | `DeleteTodoError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
| Not authorized (delete) | `DeleteTodoError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
| Email invalid | `RegistrationError::InvalidEmail(EmailValidationError)` | Email fails format validation |
| Duplicate email | `RegistrationError::DuplicateEmail` | Registration attempted with existing email |
| Invalid credentials | `AuthenticationError::InvalidCredentials` | Login failed (generic -- no info leak) |
| Invalid title | `AddTodoError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in add_todo service |

## Error Copy Convention

Domain error messages (`#[error("...")]`) describe **what invariant was violated** -- they
are written for developers and logs. User-facing error messages are **instructions** that
tell the user what to fix -- they are written for humans scanning a form.

The route/template layer translates domain errors into user-facing copy. Templates never
call `error.to_string()` on a domain error.

| Domain Error | User-Facing Copy |
|-------------|-----------------|
| `EmailValidationError::Empty` | "Enter your email address" |
| `EmailValidationError::MissingAtSymbol` | "Enter a valid email address, like name@example.com" |
| `EmailValidationError::TooLong` | "That email address is too long" |
| `PasswordError::Empty` | "Enter a password" |
| `PasswordError::TooShort` | "Your password needs at least 8 characters" |
| `PasswordError::TooLong` | "That password is too long" |
| `RegistrationError::DuplicateEmail` | "Unable to create account. If you already have an account, try signing in." |
| `AuthenticationError::InvalidCredentials` | "That email or password didn't work. Try again." |
| `TodoTitleError::Empty` | (silently ignored per US-5 -- empty submissions are not errors) |
| `TodoTitleError::TooLong` | "That title is too long (max 300 characters)" |

*Copy reviewed by Steve Krug. Update this table when adding new error types.*

---

## Type Design Principles

These principles govern how we model the domain:

1. **Parse, don't validate**: `TodoTitle::parse(input) -> Result<TodoTitle, TodoError>`
   not `fn is_valid_title(input: &str) -> bool`. Parsing produces a typed value that
   carries proof of validity. Validate once at the boundary; never re-validate inside.

2. **Make illegal states unrepresentable**: A `TodoItem` is either `Pending` or
   `Completed` -- never both, never neither. The enum enforces this at compile time.
   No boolean `is_completed` flag with an `Option<completed_at>` that could be
   inconsistent.

3. **Newtypes for everything**: `UserId` is not a bare `Uuid`. `ValidatedEmail` is
   not a bare `String`. The type system prevents mixing them up. These are zero-cost
   abstractions in Rust.

4. **Pure domain core**: Everything in `src/domain/` is pure functions and types. No
   database imports, no HTTP types, no I/O. If you need a mock to test domain logic,
   I/O has leaked into the domain.

5. **Workflows as type-safe pipelines**:
   ```
   UnvalidatedInput -> ValidatedInput -> DomainResult
   ```
   Each step's output type is the next step's input. You cannot skip validation.

## State Machine: TodoItem

```
                 add_todo()
  [nothing] ────────────────> Pending
                                 │  ^
               item.complete()   │  │  item.uncomplete()
                                 │  │
                                 v  │
                             Completed
```

Both `Pending` and `Completed` variants can be deleted (`delete_todo()`).
The `edit_todo_title()` action applies to both states.
Completion is **reversible** -- users can toggle between Pending and Completed (US-6).

## Error Copy Convention

Domain error messages (`#[error("...")]`) describe **what invariant was violated** -- they
are written for developers and logs. User-facing error messages are **instructions** that
tell the user what to fix -- they are written for humans scanning a form.

The route/template layer translates domain errors into user-facing copy. Templates never
call `error.to_string()` on a domain error.

| Domain Error | User-Facing Copy |
|-------------|-----------------|
| `EmailValidationError::Empty` | "Enter your email address" |
| `EmailValidationError::MissingAtSymbol` | "Enter a valid email address, like name@example.com" |
| `EmailValidationError::TooLong` | "That email address is too long" |
| `PasswordError::Empty` | "Enter a password" |
| `PasswordError::TooShort` | "Your password needs at least 8 characters" |
| `PasswordError::TooLong` | "That password is too long" |
| `RegistrationError::DuplicateEmail` | "Unable to create account. If you already have an account, try signing in." |
| `AuthenticationError::InvalidCredentials` | "That email or password didn't work. Try again." |
| `TodoTitleError::Empty` | (silently ignored per US-5 -- empty submissions are not errors) |
| `TodoTitleError::TooLong` | "That title is too long (max 300 characters)" |

*Copy reviewed by Steve Krug. Update this table when adding new error types.*

---

## Type Design Principles

These principles govern how we model the domain:

1. **Parse, don't validate**: `TodoTitle::parse(input) -> Result<TodoTitle, TodoError>`
   not `fn is_valid_title(input: &str) -> bool`. Parsing produces a typed value that
   carries proof of validity. Validate once at the boundary; never re-validate inside.

2. **Make illegal states unrepresentable**: A `TodoItem` is either `Pending` or
   `Completed` -- never both, never neither. The enum enforces this at compile time.
   No boolean `is_completed` flag with an `Option<completed_at>` that could be
   inconsistent.

3. **Newtypes for everything**: `UserId` is not a bare `Uuid`. `ValidatedEmail` is
   not a bare `String`. The type system prevents mixing them up. These are zero-cost
   abstractions in Rust.

4. **Pure domain core**: Everything in `src/domain/` is pure functions and types. No
   database imports, no HTTP types, no I/O. If you need a mock to test domain logic,
   I/O has leaked into the domain.

5. **Workflows as type-safe pipelines**:
   ```
   UnvalidatedInput -> ValidatedInput -> DomainResult
   ```
   Each step's output type is the next step's input. You cannot skip validation.

```
                 add_todo()
  [nothing] ────────────────> Pending

               item.complete()   │  │  item.uncomplete()
                                 │  │
                                 v  │
                             Completed
```

Both `Pending` and `Completed` variants can be deleted (`delete_todo()`).
The `edit_todo_title()` action applies to both states.
Completion is **reversible** -- users can toggle between Pending and Completed (US-6).

# Domain Glossary

Maps business terms to Rust types. This is the ubiquitous language of our todo list
application. Code should read like these definitions -- if the business says "complete
a todo," the code says `complete_todo()`.

Maintained by the mob. Changes reviewed by the domain architect (Scott Wlaschin).

---

## Core Domain Types

| Domain Term | Rust Type | Module | Notes |
|-------------|-----------|--------|-------|
| User | `User` | `domain` | An authenticated user with valid credentials |
| User ID | `UserId(Uuid)` | `domain` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a user |
| Email address | `ValidatedEmail` | `domain` | Constructed via `ValidatedEmail::parse()`; validated format, normalized to lowercase (case-insensitive per US-2) |
| Password | (not stored as domain type) | -- | Hashed with Argon2 at the boundary; the domain never sees raw passwords |
| Todo item | `TodoItem` (enum) | `domain` | Sum type: `Pending` or `Completed` variant. No boolean flags. |
| Todo item ID | `TodoItemId(Uuid)` | `domain` | Newtype wrapper; uniquely identifies a todo item |
| Todo title | `TodoTitle` | `domain` | Non-empty, max 300 chars (per US-5), trimmed; constructed via `TodoTitle::parse()` |
| Pending todo | `TodoItem::Pending { ... }` | `domain` | A todo that has not been completed; has title and created_at |
| Completed todo | `TodoItem::Completed { ... }` | `domain` | A todo that has been completed; has title, created_at, and completed_at |

## Domain Actions (Ubiquitous Language)

| Business Action | Rust Function / Method | Notes |
|-----------------|----------------------|-------|
| Register a user | `register_user()` | Service: creates user with validated email and hashed password |
| Log in | `authenticate_user()` | Service: verifies credentials; route stores user_id in session |
| Log out | `post_logout()` | Route: flushes session, redirects to login |
| Add a todo | `add_todo()` | Service: parses title, creates `TodoItem::Pending`, persists |
| View todos | `get_todos()` | Service: fetches all todos for authenticated user |
| Toggle completion | `toggle_todo_completion()` | Service: looks up todo, verifies ownership, toggles state |
| Complete a todo | `TodoItem::complete()` | Domain method: Pending -> Completed; records completion time |
| Uncomplete a todo | `TodoItem::uncomplete()` | Domain method: Completed -> Pending; drops completion time |
| Delete a todo | `delete_todo()` | Service: verifies ownership, permanently removes from DB |
| Edit a todo title | `update_todo_title()` | Service: verifies ownership, parses new title, persists |

## Domain Errors

| Error Condition | Rust Type | Notes |
|-----------------|-----------|-------|
| Title is empty | `TodoTitleError::Empty` | Todo title cannot be blank |
| Title too long | `TodoTitleError::TooLong { max, actual }` | Exceeds maximum allowed length |
| Todo not found (toggle) | `ToggleTodoError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
| Not authorized (toggle) | `ToggleTodoError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
| Todo not found (delete) | `DeleteTodoError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
| Not authorized (delete) | `DeleteTodoError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
| Email invalid | `RegistrationError::InvalidEmail(EmailValidationError)` | Email fails format validation |
| Duplicate email | `RegistrationError::DuplicateEmail` | Registration attempted with existing email |
| Invalid credentials | `AuthenticationError::InvalidCredentials` | Login failed (generic -- no info leak) |
| Invalid title (add) | `AddTodoError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in add_todo service |
| Todo not found (edit) | `UpdateTitleError::NotFound` | Referenced todo does not exist |
| Not authorized (edit) | `UpdateTitleError::Unauthorized` | User does not own the todo |
| Invalid title (edit) | `UpdateTitleError::InvalidTitle(TodoTitleError)` | Title validation failure in update_todo_title service |

## Error Copy Convention

Domain error messages (`#[error("...")]`) describe **what invariant was violated** -- they
are written for developers and logs. User-facing error messages are **instructions** that
tell the user what to fix -- they are written for humans scanning a form.

The route/template layer translates domain errors into user-facing copy. Templates never
call `error.to_string()` on a domain error.

| Domain Error | User-Facing Copy |
|-------------|-----------------|
| `EmailValidationError::Empty` | "Enter your email address" |
| `EmailValidationError::MissingAtSymbol` | "Enter a valid email address, like name@example.com" |
| `EmailValidationError::TooLong` | "That email address is too long" |
| `PasswordError::Empty` | "Enter a password" |
| `PasswordError::TooShort` | "Your password needs at least 8 characters" |
| `PasswordError::TooLong` | "That password is too long" |
| `RegistrationError::DuplicateEmail` | "Unable to create account. If you already have an account, try signing in." |
| `AuthenticationError::InvalidCredentials` | "That email or password didn't work. Try again." |
| `TodoTitleError::Empty` | (silently ignored per US-5 -- empty submissions are not errors) |
| `TodoTitleError::TooLong` | "That title is too long (max 300 characters)" |

*Copy reviewed by Steve Krug. Update this table when adding new error types.*

---

## Type Design Principles

These principles govern how we model the domain:

1. **Parse, don't validate**: `TodoTitle::parse(input) -> Result<TodoTitle, TodoError>`
   not `fn is_valid_title(input: &str) -> bool`. Parsing produces a typed value that
   carries proof of validity. Validate once at the boundary; never re-validate inside.

2. **Make illegal states unrepresentable**: A `TodoItem` is either `Pending` or

   I/O has leaked into the domain.

5. **Workflows as type-safe pipelines**:
   ```
   UnvalidatedInput -> ValidatedInput -> DomainResult
   ```
   Each step's output type is the next step's input. You cannot skip validation.

## State Machine: TodoItem

```
                 add_todo()
  [nothing] ────────────────> Pending
                                 │  ^
               item.complete()   │  │  item.uncomplete()
                                 │  │
                                 v  │
                             Completed
```

The `edit_todo_title()` action applies to both states.

content.text: All 69 tests green. Now let me send my domain review to the team-lead.

input.content:
**Domain Architecture Review -- Task #9 (Delete) and Task #20 (Edit) -- CONSENT**

Both features pass domain review. Here is what I did and found:

## Task #9 (Delete) -- Complete
The 5 delete integration tests are solid and cover all the cases I recommended: happy path with empty-state transition, 404 for nonexistent, 403 for unauthorized, unauthenticated redirect, and accessible aria-label. No domain concerns.

## Task #20 (Edit) -- In Progress, Route Wired

**What I did:**
1. **Wired up the edit route** in `startup.rs` (lines 66-69): `/todos/{id}/edit` with GET (form) + POST (submit). The imports and route handlers were already in `routes/mod.rs` and `routes/todos.rs`, but the route was not registered in the router.
2. **Added edit link** to `templates/todos.html` (lines 49-52): Each todo now has a pencil icon (✎) link with `aria-label="Edit "{{ todo.title }}""` for accessibility.
3. **Updated glossary** (`docs/glossary.md`):
   - Fixed `edit_todo_title()` -> `update_todo_title()` to match actual function name
   - Added `UpdateTitleError` variants (NotFound, Unauthorized, InvalidTitle) to error table
   - Added context-specific error copy: `TodoTitleError::Empty` in edit context maps to "Title cannot be empty" (vs silently ignored in add context per US-5)
   - Updated state machine reference

**Domain observations:**
- `update_todo_title` service correctly follows the ownership-check + parse-don't-validate pattern
- `UpdateTitleError` enum has proper variants matching the existing error convention
- The `get_edit_todo` handler reaches directly into `todo_repository::find_todo_by_id` instead of going through a service function -- minor architectural concern for MVP, noting for future cleanup
- Error copy differentiation between add (silent ignore) and edit ("Title cannot be empty") is correct -- editing is an intentional action where empty titles should be rejected

**Quality gate: 69 tests green (30 unit + 39 integration), clippy clean, fmt clean.**

Edit integration tests are still needed. I recommend the same pattern as delete: happy path edit, 404, 403, unauthenticated redirect, empty title validation (422), too-long title validation (422), and edit form displays current title.

content.text:
Excellent. Teammates added exactly the edit tests I recommended:
- `get_edit_shows_form_with_current_title` -- edit form shows current title
- `post_edit_updates_title_and_redirects` -- happy path
- `post_edit_returns_404_for_nonexistent_todo` -- 404
- `post_edit_returns_403_for_another_users_todo` -- 403
- `post_edit_with_empty_title_returns_422` -- empty title validation
- `post_edit_with_too_long_title_returns_422` -- too-long title validation
- `get_edit_redirects_to_login_when_unauthenticated` -- unauthenticated GET
- `post_edit_redirects_to_login_when_unauthenticated` -- unauthenticated POST
- `edit_link_has_accessible_label` -- a11y

That's 9 edit tests covering all the cases. The glossary is the only remaining uncommitted change, and it's purely documentation. Let me send a status update to team-lead.

input.content:
**Status Update: All edit integration tests are in and passing.**

Teammates added 9 edit tests covering all the cases I recommended: happy path, 404, 403, unauthenticated (GET+POST), empty title (422), too-long title (422), form shows current title, and accessible aria-label on edit link.

**Quality gate: 78 tests green (30 unit + 48 integration), clippy clean, fmt clean.**

Only uncommitted change is my glossary update (`docs/glossary.md`) which:
- Fixed function name `edit_todo_title()` -> `update_todo_title()` to match code
- Added `UpdateTitleError` variants to error table
- Added context-specific error copy for edit vs add

Task #20 (Edit) looks complete from a domain perspective. Both Tasks #9 and #20 have full integration test coverage. Ready for the team to review and commit the glossary update.
